metaTitle: Spine Support & Bracing | Lumbar, Cervical, Scoliosis | Avana Surgical
metaDescription: Doctor-recommended lumbar, cervical, and scoliosis bracing for spine pain, post-op recovery and curvature correction — dispatched in India by Osteokart.

hero.title: Bracing for every part of the spine.
hero.description: From lumbar support for low back pain, to cervical collars for neck recovery, to scoliosis bracing during growth — the right spine support, properly fitted, makes a meaningful difference.

conditionsHeading.title: When spine bracing makes sense
conditionsHeading.lead: Bracing is one of the most evidence-supported non-surgical tools for spine care.

conditions.title: Low back pain
conditions.description: Lumbar bracing offloads stressed structures and supports recovery from mechanical low back pain and disc-related symptoms.

conditions.title: Scoliosis (during growth)
conditions.description: Modern scoliosis bracing has strong evidence for slowing or stopping curve progression in adolescents.

conditions.title: Post-spine surgery
conditions.description: After discectomy, fusion, or endoscopic spine surgery, the right brace protects the surgical site while healing happens.

conditions.title: Cervical support
conditions.description: Cervical collars provide support for neck pain, post-trauma recovery, and post-surgical immobilisation.

productsHeading.title: Spine bracing solutions
productsHeading.lead: Lumbar, cervical, and scoliosis bracing selected for clinical performance and Indian patient fit.

testimonials.name: Fawzul Hidhaya's Mother
testimonials.testimonial: My daughter was diagnosed with scoliosis at 11. After years of exercises, the curve reached 32°. Surgery was advised — but with Spinamic, before the brace it was 35°, and with the brace it reduced to 25°.

seoContent.title: Spine bracing — what works, when
seoContent.intro: The spine is more than a backbone — it's the central support of the body. The right brace, used at the right time, can change recovery and quality of life.

sections.heading: Lumbar bracing for low back pain
sections.body: Lumbar bracing offloads stressed structures, limits painful motion, and gives the spine the right environment to heal. Most effective when paired with physiotherapy.

sections.heading: Scoliosis: evidence-based bracing
sections.body: Scoliosis bracing during growth has strong clinical evidence for slowing or stopping curve progression. Results depend heavily on age, curve type, and how consistently the brace is worn.

sections.heading: Cervical support
sections.body: Cervical collars range from soft foam support for neck strain to rigid post-trauma immobilisers. Your specialist will recommend the right level of support.

seoContent.disclaimer: Educational content. Always consult a spine specialist before starting any bracing programme.

faq.question: How long do I wear a lumbar brace?
faq.answer: Depends on the indication. For acute pain, often 1–4 weeks. For chronic low back pain, intermittent use during high-demand activities. For post-surgery, follow your surgeon's protocol.

faq.question: Will the brace weaken my back muscles?
faq.answer: Largely a myth when bracing is paired with physiotherapy. Bracing supports recovery; rehab strengthens the back.

faq.question: How long does scoliosis bracing take to work?
faq.answer: Meaningful curve stabilisation or reduction usually takes 12–24 months of consistent wear during the growth period. Compliance is the single biggest factor.

cta.title: Spine pain is personal — let us help you choose right.
